questions.question: The part of the Indian Constitution that contains Directive Principles of State Policy.
The articles under which Directive Principles are stated.
The country whose constitution inspired the Directive Principles of State Policy in India.
The term used for principles that cannot be enforced by any court of law.
The Gandhian principle that means 'welfare for all'.
The constitutional amendment that added the duty to protect the environment as a Directive Principle.
The development programme launched in 1952 to raise the standard of living.
The system abolished to distribute surplus land among the landless poor.
The article under which the government established boards to promote cottage industries.
The type of local self-government body given powers under Gandhian Directive Principles.
The method of settling international disputes recommended under General Directive Principles.
The type of code that the state shall aim to establish for all citizens.
The social practice made a punishable offence as an impact of Directive Principles.
